summary: Monster Playing In The Band jam and stellar Brokedown Palace
blurb: The second set delivers a massive Playing In The Band that builds into a tight Truckin' > Dark Star sequence, while the first set shines with Bird Song and Casey Jones. Jerry's soaring guitar and A‑plus vocals dominate, and Keith’s piano adds texture. Overall the show is praised for its energy and tight interplay despite minor hiss in the recording.
review: On October 26, 1972 at Cincinnati Music Hall the Dead rolled out a setlist that still feels fresh decades later. The first set was a solid romp through classics – The Promised Land, Sugaree, Bird Song and a bright Casey Jones – but it’s the second set that truly lifts this night into legend territory. A monster Playing In The Band erupts early, its soaring guitar lines and thunderous drumming setting up a seamless segue into Deal, then into an ecstatic Truckin' > Dark Star stretch that never loses its momentum before closing with a joyous Sugar Magnolia and Sing Me Back Home. Highlights also include a beautifully rendered (though abruptly cut) Brokedown Palace and the ever‑reliable Bird Song from set one. Reviewers consistently note Jerry’s scorching solos, A+ vocal delivery, and his “wawa” that drives the jams; Phil’s bass is described as powerful – sending you into “stupidville” when it cuts through the mix – while Keith’s piano/Rhodes work adds subtle pedal‑effect color. Billy Kreutzmann’s drumming propels the band forward, even if his bass drum occasionally steps to the front of the mix. Minor hiss and an early board cut on Brokedown Palace are the only blemishes in an otherwise crisp soundboard capture, making this performance a must‑listen for any Deadhead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings share a uniformly positive tone, repeatedly praising the monster Playing In The Band, soaring Jerry Garcia work, and overall tight band interplay. Multiple sources highlight the same standout songs (Playing In The Band, Brokedown Palace, Truckin', Dark Star) and note consistent strengths across the set, leading to a strong positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Good overall soundboard capture with clear mix, slight hiss only; includes the iconic Playing In The Band and a solid Brokedown Palace despite an end cut, making it the most complete and listenable version.

key_highlights: Monster Playing In The Band jam that builds and peaks in intensity
Stellar Brokedown Palace performance (despite abrupt ending)
Tight Truckin' > Dark Star segue driving the second‑set energy

band_performance.Jerry: Soaring lead work and A+ vocals; scorching solos and his ‘wawa’ drive the jams.
band_performance.Phil: Powerful bass lines that send you into ‘stupidville’; brilliant when audible despite occasional board issues.
band_performance.Bob: Vocals noted as weaker on a few numbers such as Sugar Magnolia, but otherwise solid rhythm work.
band_performance.Billy: Drums drive the train with stellar energy; bass drum can be overly prominent at times.
